investment_thesis.summary: Strong balance sheet (net cash) and steady ~12-15% growth in cables & FMEG with improving margin profile position Finolex Cables for consistent compounding at reasonable valuations

Associated Alcohols is a contrarian play on India's spirit consumption growth, trading at 19x P/E (vs beverages sector 33x) with 46% gross margins, 16% EBITDA margins, and a proven premiumization strategy (Nicobar gin, Hillfort whiskey, planned tequila) ₹ the stock has corrected -44% from ₹1,478 peak despite margin expansion, positioning it as a high-conviction value opportunity for investors who believe India's young demographic (median age 28, rising disposable income) will drive 30-35% proprietary brand volume growth and justify ₹1,200-1,400 fair value by FY27-28

Kaynes Technology is a structurally compelling long-term EMS-to-Semiconductor player (₹90,722 Cr order book, 56.8% revenue CAGR FY22-FY25) undergoing transformational capex (₹3,300 Cr OSAT + ₹352 Cr Bhopal + HDI PCBs + ₹5,000 Cr Tamil Nadu MoU) but facing near-term headwinds: Q3 FY26 guidance miss (₹804 Cr vs ₹1,300 Cr guided = 38% shortfall), FY26 guidance cut ₹4,100 Cr (from ₹4,400 Cr due to ₹300 Cr Kavach railway order deferral), elevated WC (139 days), and negative FCF 9M ₹ trading at 64x FY27E P/E vs sector 25-30x = 50-60% OVERVALUED despite PM-inaugurated Sanand OSAT representing 10-15% of FY27 revenue potential by FY28 if executed. Wait for: (1) Working capital normalization to <100 days, (2) OSAT achieving ₹100+ Cr quarterly revenue run-rate (target Jan 2026 achieved?), (3) FY27 guidance with 20%+ growth confirmation, (4) Stock re-rating to 30-35x = ₹2,800-3,200 fair value post-execution proof
Deep value play trading at ~0.9x P/B with strong balance sheet and counter-cyclical fleet expansion positioning it for upside in next shipping cycle
Dominant aerospace PSU with ₹90,000+ Cr order book (~3x revenue) and stable ~25% EBITDA margins offers strong earnings visibility with 12-15% growth
High-growth niche in eco-friendly packaging (~20-25% growth, ~20-25% EBITDA margins) but limited disclosure and premium valuation reduce conviction.

Interarch Building Solutions is a compelling growth-at-reasonable-price opportunity in India's 13% CAGR Pre-Engineered Building (PEB) sector, with 43.7% Q3 YoY revenue growth (+₹523 Cr record high), ₹1,685 Cr order book providing 9-12 month visibility, ₹100 Cr QIP-funded capex to unlock 15-20% CAGR through FY28, and emerging export opportunity (+₹13 Cr Q3 run-rate) ₹ trading at 16-18x FY27E P/E vs analyst consensus 25x on 40%+ earnings growth, representing 20-35% downside risk to valuation floor but 50-100% upside if execution delivers
Stable ~13-15% margin legacy battery business with ~25-30% market share is offset by ₹9,500-10,000 Cr high-risk Li-ion capex with delayed earnings visibility

FY27 15-20% biosimilar growth + GLP-1 generics market entry + ₹300 Cr interest savings create 40-60% upside potential by FY27-28 if execution delivers on merger completion and Semaglutide regulatory approvals; current 13-15x FY27E EV/EBITDA undervalues growth trajectory.
Strong ₹20,000+ Cr order book and ~20% growth visibility supported by defence indigenization, but rich valuation (~45-50x P/E) and execution delays cap near-term upside

Rossell Techsys is a structurally compelling high-growth aerospace & defence play with exceptional 99% 9M revenue growth (+₹343 Cr), emerging semiconductor TAM entry (₹10+ Cr in debut quarter), ₹700 Cr firm order book + ₹700 Cr bid pipeline, and Lockheed Martin supplier recognition ₹ BUT trading at absurd 326x P/E + 19.3x P/B despite low 13% margins, negative FCF (₹-42 Cr FY25 despite ₹11 Cr profit), 5.3% ROE and 6% ROCE = severe value destruction metrics that make current valuation a 40-60% downside risk to fair value ₹400-500/share even if growth continues 50%+ through FY27.
Turnaround-driven margin expansion (~8-10% EBITDA target) is promising but modest ~8-10% growth and heavy US exposure limit near-term rerating

Aggressive de-leveraging and FY27 recovery optionality present asymmetric upside (20-30%), but near-term execution risk and sector macro headwinds demand proof before upgrade; buy on dips below ₹30/share.

Sagility India is a high-growth, margin-stable healthcare BPM platform (₹19,712M Q3 revenue +35.7% YoY, 26% EBITDA margins, 50% ROCE) benefiting from structural US healthcare transformation (payers shifting to risk-bearing Medicare Advantage models) with successful BroadPath acquisition integration expanding seasonal OE volumes to 5.5% of annual revenue, GenAI automation (32 deployed use cases) protecting margins despite 19% employee cost inflation, and conservative FY26 22.5% guidance (vs 30%+ run-rate) ₹ trading at 50x FY26E P/E offers 20-25% upside to ₹650-700 if growth sustains 20%+ and margins hold 25%+ through normalized cycles.

ideaForge is India's only DGQA-certified indigenous tactical UAV manufacturer sitting on a record ₹368 Cr open order book and riding a post-Operation-Sindoor ₹20,000 Cr government drone procurement wave ₹ the structural bull case is unimpeachable ₹ but with Q4 FY26 requiring a 5x quarterly revenue step-up to deliver management guidance and the stock already -45% from its peak at ₹285, conviction requires proof of execution on May 12 before upgrading to BUY
Large ₹50,000+ Cr order book (~3-4x revenue) provides visibility but low 10-12% EBITDA margins and high execution risk make current valuation unattractive, especially vs ₹478 avg cost.

HDFC Bank's NIM trough at 3.38% is the single biggest valuation discount driver; with 40-50 bps of deposit repricing STILL TO COME as legacy term deposits mature through FY27-28, every 10 bps NIM recovery adds ~₹1,600 Cr NII annually ₹ making this a 25-40% upside story at 2.5x P/B vs historical 3.5-4x, backstopped by India's best-in-class GNPA of 1.15% and a ₹31 trillion deposit franchise deepening toward retail granularity

Cigniti's 7-quarter revenue growth streak + operating margin expansion from 6.56% trough to 17.93% record high validates a structural recovery story; at 16-23x P/E vs sector 27-28x, the market is underpricing BlueSwan AI platform optionality + Coforge cross-sell pipeline that could drive ₹3,000+ Cr revenue by FY28 from current ₹2,200 Cr base

CDSL's duopoly moat and 80% market share provide durability, but premium 45-58x P/E valuation leaves no margin for error as margin compression (530 bps YoY due to 25% cost inflation) challenges near-term earnings; avoid until Q4 FY26 guidance clarifies margin stabilization path.

Titan Biotech is a debt-free, 30-year legacy pharma/biotech ingredients player (₹56.51 Cr Q3 revenue +47.64% YoY, PAT margin expanding 540 bps to 13.93%) with fortress global distribution (100+ countries, India 80-85% revenue base) and quality moat (cGMP, ISO 13485, FSSAI, CE certified) capitalizing on structural pharma/biotech TAM growth (15-20% CAGR India, 25%+ global) at valuation of 18-20x FY27E P/E offering 25-35% upside to ₹125-140 if FY27 guidance confirms 25-30% growth + 13-14% PAT margin normalization, despite near-term risk of Q3's exceptional 107% PAT growth moderating to base 30-40% run-rate.

Avanti Feeds is India's largest shrimp feed manufacturer hitting a rare convergence of positive catalysts: (1) Indo-US tariff reversal (50% ₹ 18%) unlocking US market, (2) EU market access accelerating, (3) easing raw material costs expanding margins, (4) shrimp processing segment +37% YoY profitability ₹ creating a double-tailwind scenario with EBITDA margins expanding from 8% (Q3 FY25) to 13% (Q3 FY26) while trading at reasonable 15-17x P/E with 260% ROCE and 20.5% ROE, positioning it as a high-conviction play on India's ₹15,000+ Cr shrimp industry hitting an inflection point

ACME Solar is a high-growth renewable energy IPP with India's first-mover 2+ GWh BESS operational capacity and record 1,050 MW YTD PPA pipeline targeting 10 GW by 2030 ₹ riding a structural tailwind in India's 500 GW renewable target ₹ but with interest coverage at a dangerously thin 1.15x (EBIT/Interest) and net debt to EBITDA at 4.2x, the equity story is entirely dependent on flawless FY27 execution (1.5 GW + 4 GWh BESS commissioning) to improve coverage above 1.5x; making this a HIGH-CONVICTION SECTOR BET disguised as a LEVERAGE TIME-BOMB

POWERGRID.asset_name: Power Grid Corporation of India Ltd

asset_info.name: Power Grid Corporation of India Ltd

investment_thesis.summary: TruAlt Bioenergy is India's largest integrated biofuel player capitalizing on structural energy transition (Government e-blending mandates, SAF aviation fuel incentives, CBG renewable gas push) with fortress government relationships (GAIL partnership, OMC license), 5 operational ethanol plants at 95% capacity utilization (+72% Q3 revenue growth to ₹730.86 Cr), and ₹3,200-3,750 Cr capex pipeline (24 CBG units + 100 MLPA SAF facility) offering 20-25% CAGR FY26-28 with margin recovery to 25-28% EBITDA as capex phase completes, despite near-term margin compression (18.4% Q3 from 29.9% prior) warranting accumulation below ₹2,200 on FY27-28 execution visibility.

MapMyIndia is a deep-tech geospatial/location intelligence platform navigating near-term government project execution headwinds (Q3 -18.2% revenue decline, EBITDA margin -780 bps) but structurally well-positioned in 25%+ CAGR India geospatial/IoT TAM with fortress balance sheet (₹642.8 Cr net cash), ₹1,770.7 Cr order book (3-4 year visibility), and IoT segment surging 44% 9M growth ₹ BUT valuation at 29x FY28E P/E on conservative 12-15% revenue CAGR assumes flawless execution (government project volatility proven risky in Q3) and IoT margin expansion from 12.4% to 18-20% (unproven), offering limited 5-10% upside to ₹850-900 fair value unless Q4 execution + FY27 growth confirmation materialize.
